From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

National Sports Centres in England

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This article **relies largely or entirely on a [single source](/wiki/Wikipedia:Articles_with_a_single_source "Wikipedia:Articles with a single source")**. Relevant discussion may be found on the [talk page](/wiki/Talk:National_Sports_Centre_(Sport_England) "Talk:National Sports Centre (Sport England)"). Please help [improve this article](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=National_Sports_Centre_(Sport_England)&action=edit) by [introducing citations to additional sources](/wiki/Help:Referencing_for_beginners "Help:Referencing for beginners"). *Find sources:* ["National Sports Centre" Sport England](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22National+Sports+Centre%22+Sport+England) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22National+Sports+Centre%22+Sport+England+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22National+Sports+Centre%22+Sport+England&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22National+Sports+Centre%22+Sport+England+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22National+Sports+Centre%22+Sport+England) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22National+Sports+Centre%22+Sport+England&acc=on&wc=on) *(September 2011)* |

There are three **National Sports Centres** as part of [Sport England](/wiki/Sport_England "Sport England")'s strategy to create elite [English](/wiki/England "England") world class sporting talent:

* [Bisham Abbey](/wiki/Bisham_Abbey#Sports_centre "Bisham Abbey")
* [Lilleshall](/wiki/Lilleshall_Hall#Sports_centre "Lilleshall Hall")
* [Plas y Brenin](/wiki/Plas_y_Brenin "Plas y Brenin")

Each centre provides elite athletes with a range of specialist facilities, equipment, expertise and residential accommodation suitable for training and competition.[[1]](#cite_note-1) The centres also offer full facilities for the general public including conferencing and banqueting, as well as beginners and improvers programmes in most of the activities that take place at each site.

Former centres were located at [Crystal Palace](/wiki/Crystal_Palace_National_Sports_Centre "Crystal Palace National Sports Centre") and [Holme Pierrepont](/wiki/Holme_Pierrepont_National_Watersports_Centre "Holme Pierrepont National Watersports Centre").
